nodes.title: Install MongoDB
widgets_values:
To set up the backend for your database nodes (like **ZMongo**), you'll need the **MongoDB Community Edition**. As of 2026, the current stable version is **8.x**, which includes native vector search support—perfect for AI-driven projects.

### 🌐 Official Download Portal
The easiest way to get the correct version for any system is the **MongoDB Download Center**:
👉 **[Download MongoDB Community Server](https://www.mongodb.com/try/download/community)**

---

### 💻 Platform-Specific Instructions

#### **Windows**
For Windows 10 or 11, it is recommended to use the **MSI Installer**.
1.  Select **Windows** as the platform on the download page.
2.  Download and run the `.msi` file.
3.  **Pro Tip:** During installation, ensure "**Install MongoDB as a Service**" is checked so it starts automatically with your computer.

#### **macOS**
The standard way to install on Mac is via **Homebrew**. Open your terminal and run:
```bash
brew tap mongodb/brew
brew install mongodb-community@8.0
```
To start the database: `brew services start mongodb-community@8.0`.

#### **Linux**
MongoDB provides native packages (`.deb` and `.rpm`) for major distributions like **Ubuntu, Debian, and RedHat**.
* **Ubuntu/Debian:** Use the `.deb` package.
* **RedHat/CentOS:** Use the `.rpm` package.
* Detailed guides for each distro can be found in the [Official Linux Installation Docs](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/administration/install-on-linux/).

#### **Docker (Quickest Setup)**
If you don't want to install it directly on your OS, you can run a containerized version with one command:
```bash
docker pull mongodb/mongodb-community-server
docker run --name mongodb -d -p 27017:27017 mongodb/mongodb-community-server
```

---

### 🔍 Don't Forget the GUI
While the server runs in the background, you'll likely want **MongoDB Compass**. It’s the official visual tool for exploring your data, and it is usually offered as an optional checkbox during the Windows installation.

* **Download Compass:** [https://www.mongodb.com/try/download/compass](https://www.mongodb.com/try/download/compass)

> [!NOTE]
> Since you are using **ComfyUI-ZMongo**, ensure your connection string in ComfyUI matches your local instance (usually `mongodb://127.0.0.1:27017`) once the service is running.
